title: Status Lines, Informational Responses, Content Rules, and Response Parsing · Runtime Contract: Informational Response Loop
summary: A client parser accepts malformed status codes, stops at 100 Continue, assigns content to HEAD and 204, treats successful CONNECT as ordinary HTTP, and retries after an incomplete response. Planned artifact: Add and verify the informational response loop increment in response control-data and content decision parser. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Process zero or more permitted informational responses before the final response with bounded count and protocol-specific handling.
Parse HTTP version, three-digit status code, optional reason phrase, and CRLF while treating the reason phrase as non-authoritative.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Informational Response Loop · Theory Informational Response Loop
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 6030 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound informational responses and transition HEAD, no-content, and CONNECT responses correctly; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then change certificate identity while ALPN stays valid and reject the connection. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Process zero or more permitted informational responses before the final response with bounded count and protocol-specific handling.
